titleStmt.title: SII 15.695: original edition by P. B. Desai, Dines Chandra Sircar and G. S. Gai
No. 695. (B.K. No. 92 of 1926-27.) MUḶGUND, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a pillar in the Pārśvanātha temple.

It states that the Jaina preceptor Sahasrakīrti, disciple of Lalitakīrti, of Desigaṇa, Pustaka-gachchha and Hanasōge-anvaya, stayed unshaken inside the Pārśvanātha Jinālaya which was set on fire by the Mohammadans and was burnt to death. He thus vindicated the greatness of the Jaina faith.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrīmatpharamagaṁbhīraḥ syādvādāmēphalāṁchanaṁ jīyā
trailōkyanāthasya śāsanaṁ jinaśāsanaṁThese two lines are engraved in ṇāgari characters.
siḍilaśrī jinapārśvanāthana mahā
caityālayaṁ durmmukharkkaḍumūrkkhartturuka
rssuḍalke tolegaḷkaṁbhaṁgaḷuṁ bhittigaḷkeḷaya
lkaṁḍu suvajradaṁtkabalupaṁtuṁ tāḷdiralma
ḍidaṁ poḍavīkhyāta sahasrakīrttiyatipaṁ dēśīga
ṇāgrēsaraṁ . 1 . hanasōgānvayajāta pustaka
mahāgacchēśvaraṁ satkrusāvanividvajjanaka
lpabhūjanabhayaṁ dēśīgaṇāṁbhōdhiva
rddhanacaṁdraṁ yatiśēkharaṁ lalitakīrttyā
cāryyasaṁnaṁdanaṁ vinayāṁbōdhi saha
srakīrttiyatipaṁ mārīcapaṁcānanaṁ . 2 .
nalaviṁ śrījinadharmmamaṁ bhuvanadoḷpratī
tiyaṁ māḍi nirmmaḷacāritranivāsanāgiThe inscription is incomplete.
Digital edition of SII 15.695 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
